title: indexOf checks should not be for positive numbers
description: Most checks against an indexOf value should compare it with -1 or use >= 0, because 0 is a valid index representing the first element. Checking against > 0 ignores the first element, which is likely a bug unless you're specifically looking for later occurrences. If your intent is merely to check the presence of an element, consider using includes() instead for better readability.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Use >= 0 or !== -1 instead of > 0 when checking indexOf results to avoid missing the first element.
Comparing indexOf result to positive numbers may miss earlier occurrences. Use >= 0 or !== -1 for existence checks.
